perturbation.original_text: to refrain, directly or indirectly, during the Term from having any interest in or association with any business which competes with Sweetskinz pneumatic colorized tire products or any other future Products produced by Sweetskinz. Such entities shall include any related entity in which Mellet or his immediate family is a shareholder, director, officer, employee, partner, proprietor, joint venturer, consultant or otherwise, anywhere worldwide.
perturbation.changed_text: to refrain, directly or indirectly, during the Term from having any interest in or association with any business which competes with Sweetskinz pneumatic colorized tire products or any other future Goods manufactured by Sweetskinz. Such entities shall include any related entity in which Mellet or his immediate family is a shareholder, director, officer, employee, partner, proprietor, joint venturer, consultant or otherwise, anywhere worldwide.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Products' with 'Goods' introduces a contradiction. The agreement refers to 'Products' in relation to Mellet's inventions and assignments. By using 'Goods' instead, it becomes unclear whether the non-compete extends to inventions or is limited to manufactured items, narrowing the scope of non-compete
perturbation.contradicted_text: any idea, concept, technique, invention, creation, copyrightable or patentable work ("Product") relating to pneumatic colorized tires or other products sold or developed by Sweetskinz, that Mellet is instrumental in creating, developing or producing, or helping to create, develop or produce, during the time that Mellet is employed by Sweetskinz shall be considered owned by Sweetskinz, whether conceived by Mellet individually or jointly, on or off Sweetskinz's premises. Mellet hereby irrevocably assigns such Product to Sweetskinz and authorizes Sweetskinz to execute on Mellet’s behalf such documents as it may deem necessary to confirm and protect its interests therein. However, Mellet may develop products which do not in any material way compete with any Product of Sweeetskinz so long as such development is of no cost to Sweetskinz, is done after “working hours” (defined as 45-hour work week) and does not interfere with Mellet’s responsibilities as the Chief Technology Officer of Sweetskinz. Mellet acknowledges that this Section 2 is reasonable, among other things, because Sweetskinz anticipates selling its products on all continents of the world and developing relationships with manufacturers, wholesalers and retailers on such continents
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Products' to 'Goods' in the modified text creates a contradiction because the original text defines 'Product' broadly to include inventions and creations, not just manufactured items. The contradicted text uses 'Product' to refer to Mellet's intellectual property. By narrowing the scope to 'Goods,' the modified text implies that the non-compete only applies to manufactured items, thus creating a loophole for Mellet to compete with inventions, which contradicts the intention of the original agreement and the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Mellet hereby irrevocably assigns such Product to Sweetskinz and authorizes Sweetskinz to execute on Mellet’s behalf such documents as it may deem necessary to confirm and protect its interests therein.
perturbation.changed_text: Mellet hereby irrevocably assigns such Inventions to Sweetskinz and authorizes Sweetskinz to execute on Mellet’s behalf such documents as it may deem necessary to confirm and protect its interests therein.
perturbation.explanation: The document refers to 'Product', but switching to 'Inventions' makes the earlier and later definition differ, creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: to refrain, directly or indirectly, during the Term from having any interest in or association with any business which competes with Sweetskinz pneumatic colorized tire products or any other future Products produced by Sweetskinz. Such entities shall include any related entity in which Mellet or his immediate family is a shareholder, director, officer, employee, partner, proprietor, joint venturer, consultant or otherwise, anywhere worldwide.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Product', which aligns with the contradicted text's mention of 'future Products produced by Sweetskinz'. By changing 'Product' to 'Inventions' in the modified text, a contradiction is created. The contradicted text restricts Mellet's association with businesses competing with Sweetskinz's 'Products'. If 'Inventions' and 'Products' are not perfectly synonymous, the restriction's scope becomes unclear. The modification introduces a misalignment in terminology, leading to uncertainty regarding the scope of the non-compete clause, which falls under the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees at all times during the Term to hold in the strictest confidence, and not use, except for the benefit of the Parties or to disclose, transfer or reveal, directly or indirectly to any person or entity any confidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees at all times during the Period to hold in the strictest confidence, and not use, except for the benefit of the Parties or to disclose, transfer or reveal, directly or indirectly to any person or entity any sensitive data.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Term' to 'Period' and 'confidential information' to 'sensitive data' creates a contradiction. 'Term' is defined as a specific timeframe but 'Period' isn't, making the confidentiality obligations ambiguous. Furthermore, 'confidential information' is broad whereas 'sensitive data' often refers to personal or financial details. It is no longer clear what information Mellet must keep confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXHIBIT 10.8 AGREEMENT CONCERNING NON-DISCLOSURE/NON-CIRCUMVENTION It is understood that this is a Proprietary Information and Non-Competition Agreement with regards to Yann Mellet (“Mellet”) and Sweetskinz, Inc., a Pennsylvania corporation (“Sweetskinz”). Mellet and Sweetskinz shall be sometimes collectively be referred to as “Parties". It is understood further that changes to this Agreement must be in writing and acknowledged by the Parties. The Agreement contains material restrictions on Mellet’s right to (i) compete with Sweetskinz, or (ii) disclose or use information learned or developed by Mellet through his association with or as an employee of Sweetskinz from the beginning of time through April 4, 2016 (“Term”).
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Term' with 'Period' and 'confidential information' with 'sensitive data'. The original text's 'Term' is defined in the contradicted text as a specific timeframe (from the beginning of time through April 4, 2016), providing a clear duration for the non-disclosure agreement. The changed text's 'Period' lacks this definition, creating ambiguity. Furthermore, the change from 'confidential information' to 'sensitive data' narrows the scope of protected information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text because Mellet's obligation to protect all information learned during his association with Sweetskinz (the Term) is now limited to only sensitive data during an undefined period, thus weakening and contradicting the scope of the original agreement. This is a strong enough contradiction to cause potential legal disputes.

perturbation.original_text: If Sweetskinz so elects, Sweetskinz shall be entitled, in addition to all other remedies available, including but not limited to actual, compensatory and punitive damages, or to specifically enforce the performance by Mellet and to enjoin the violation by Mellet of any provision hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: If Sweetskinz so chooses, Sweetskinz shall be entitled, in addition to all other resolutions available, including but not limited to actual, compensatory and exemplary damages, or to specifically enforce the performance by Mellet and to enjoin the transgression by Mellet of any provision hereof.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'elects' to 'chooses', 'remedies' to 'resolutions', 'punitive' to 'exemplary', and 'violation' to 'transgression' creates legal ambiguity. 'Remedies' and 'resolutions' are not synonymous in law; 'punitive' and 'exemplary' might have slightly different legal interpretations, and 'violation' and 'transgression' don't mean exactly the same thing. This makes it less enforceable due to interpretation issues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is agreed that if any legal action or other proceeding is brought for the enforcement of this Agreement, or because of an alleged dispute, breach, default or misrepresentation in connection with any provision of this Agreement the successful Party or Parties, as the case may be, shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorney’s fees and other costs incurred in that proceeding in addition to any other relief or judgment to which it may be entitled.
perturbation.justification: The changed text uses different terminology ('chooses' instead of 'elects', 'resolutions' instead of 'remedies', 'exemplary' instead of 'punitive', and 'transgression' instead of 'violation') which introduces legal ambiguity as explained in the perturbation explanation. This directly impacts the enforceability of the agreement. The contradicted text refers to 'enforcement of this Agreement', and the changes weaken this enforcement due to potential interpretation issues. As stated in the Perturbation Definitions, 'Misaligned terminology also leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations'.
